steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two independent clauses: ‘dates back to the 3rd century BCE’ and ‘the poem's heroic narrative still captivates modern readers.’ The second clause contrasts with the first (ancient vs. still modern), so the connector must join two independent clauses and signal contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '3rd century BCE,': A comma alone cannot join two independent clauses — this creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — '3rd century BCE yet,': Placing ‘yet’ before the comma puts the conjunction inside the first clause rather than between them; the second clause is then left unconnected. ✗
  • Choice C — '3rd century BCE, yet': Comma + coordinating conjunction correctly joins two independent clauses, and ‘yet’ signals the intended contrast. ✓
  • Choice D — '3rd century BCE.': A period would force ‘the’ to start a new sentence with a lowercase letter (capitalization error), and it removes the contrast signal that ‘yet’ provides between the ancient origin and modern appeal. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
